Title: Evolutionary routes to biochemical innovation revealed by integrative analysis of a plant-defense related specialized metabolic pathway

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a trichome-expressed BAHD acyltransferase for engineering acylsugar structural diversity and defense chemistry in Solanaceae or heterologous systems; likely best deployed with other upstream/downstream ASATs and adequate acyl-CoA supply. Application should consider enzyme donor promiscuity and species/context dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers short/medium branched acyl groups from acyl-CoAs to sucrose to form mono-acylsucrose, inferred mainly at the R2 position of the pyranose ring.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates Salpiglossis acylsugar biosynthesis; knockdown reduces total acylsugar accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trichome-enriched RNA-seq candidate discovery; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assays with sucrose and acyl-CoAs; LC/MS product profiling and co-elution comparisons; VIGS knockdown in planta; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Commits substrate into pathway and directly forms the first acylated intermediate leading to acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants were sampled/grown under varying developmental and environmental conditions; VIGS phenotypes were consistent across replicate experiments despite condition differences.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Trichome-high transcript in Salpiglossis stem/petiole trichomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product position was inferred from CID fragmentation and co-migration with PaASAT1 product; enzyme accepts multiple acyl-CoA donors, indicating donor promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core acylsucrose assembly on the sucrose pyranose ring
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly reported; altered acylsugar levels may affect defense chemistry and could shift acyl-CoA usage.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Sucrose; acyl-CoAs including aiC5 and aiC6, also multiple donors tested
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Early pathway entry step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsASAT1 was identified among trichome-high BAHD genes in Salpiglossis. 2. In vitro it acylated sucrose to produce mono-acylsugars, primarily at the pyranose R2 position. 3. VIGS knockdown reduced acylsugar levels, validating its in planta role as the first pathway enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes / vegetative tissue surface exudates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense-related specialized metabolites; likely contribute to protective surface chemistry against herbivores/pathogens.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with SsASAT1, SsASAT3 and downstream ASATs to build di-acylsucrose-producing pathways and diversify acylation patterns in metabolic engineering. Care is needed because pathway perturbation can cause unexpected accumulation of lower-mass intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to the mono-acylsucrose product of SsASAT1 to generate di-acylated sucrose, likely at the R4 position of the pyranose ring.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of mono-acylsucrose to higher acylated acylsugars; knockdown decreases overall acylsugars and shifts profiles toward lower molecular weight species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Trichome-enriched RNA-seq; recombinant expression and in vitro assays using mono-acylsucrose acceptor; LC/MS co-elution and fragmentation; failure to acylate sucrose; VIGS knockdown with metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for later tri-/tetra-/penta-acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS replicate experiments were conducted under different growth/agro-infiltration conditions, but phenotypes were consistent.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Positive mode fragmentation suggested acylation on the same ring as SsASAT1. Knockdown caused complex phenotype, with decreases in higher molecular weight acylsugars and increases in some lower molecular weight species, implying network compensation or additional activities.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core acylsucrose assembly on sucrose pyranose ring
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pathway perturbation causes unexpected metabolite rebalancing and accumulation of lower-mass intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Mono-acylsucrose generated by SsASAT1; aiC5-CoA and related acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsASAT2 acylates the mono-acylated SsASAT1 product to form di-acylsucrose and does not acylate sucrose directly. 2. Product behavior matched PaASAT2 rather than SlASAT2, supporting positional assignment at pyranose R4. 3. VIGS reduced total acylsugars and altered chain-length distribution, confirming in planta function.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes / vegetative tissue surface exudates

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for installing the third pyranose-ring acylation step and tuning chain composition, especially aiC6 incorporation; should be combined with earlier ASATs and downstream furanose-ring acyltransferases. Caution: knockdown can redirect flux to unusual tri-/tetra-acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers aiC6 from aiC6-CoA to di-acylsucrose, adding the third acyl chain to the pyranose ring, inferred at the R3 position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Builds tri-acylsucrose intermediates required for major Salpiglossis acylsugars; silencing causes accumulation of normally low-abundance tri-acylsugars and derived tetra-acylsugars, indicating rerouted flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay with di-acylated acceptor; donor specificity tests; positional inference using PaASAT4 follow-up assay and LC/MS; VIGS knockdown and metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor for furanose-ring acylation steps leading to major tetra-/penta-/hexa-acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SsASAT3 used aiC6-CoA but not aiC5-CoA under tested conditions, despite presence of some in planta compounds consistent with alternative chain patterns. Knockdown phenotype implies another uncharacterized enzyme can acylate accumulated di-acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux redirection upon loss leads to novel intermediate accumulation, reflecting pathway plasticity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsASAT3 converts di-acylsucrose to tri-acylsucrose by adding aiC6 at the pyranose R3 position. 2. Position was supported by successful downstream R6 acylation by PaASAT4. 3. VIGS caused accumulation of atypical tri- and tetra-acylsugars, indicating SsASAT3 is a core node controlling metabolite flow.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes / vegetative tissue surface exudates

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for late-pathway acetylation steps that generate penta-/hexa-acylsugar diversity; useful for synthetic biology aimed at altering terminal acylsugar structures. Caution: enzyme shows acceptor promiscuity and weak alternative pyranose-ring acetylation, so product profile may depend strongly on substrate context and accession.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers acetyl groups (C2) from acetyl-CoA to tri-, tetra- and penta-acylsucroses, mainly on the furanose ring and weakly also on the pyranose ring for some penta-acylsucroses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes downstream acetylation to produce major penta-acylsugars and some hexa-acylsugars; knockdown causes accumulation of tri- and tetra-acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro assays with purified tri-/tetra-/penta-acylsucrose acceptors; LC/MS co-migration with plant metabolites; positive mode fragmentation; VIGS knockdown and metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for terminal/downstream modification of acylsugars and formation of abundant penta-acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite showing SsASAT4-, SsASAT5- and SsASAT6-like activities in vitro, authors designate it SsASAT5 because its major products match abundant in planta penta-acylsugars. Weak pyranose-ring acetylation conflicts with most NMR-characterized Salpiglossis structures but may reflect accession-specific variation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late furanose-ring acetylation branch of acylsucrose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may create off-target structural isomers in engineering contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Late-pathway terminal determinant for abundant penta-acylsugars; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsASAT5 acetylates multiple downstream acylsucrose intermediates, mainly on the furanose ring. 2. In vitro products co-migrated with major plant metabolites, supporting physiological relevance. 3. VIGS knockdown caused tri-/tetra-acylsugar accumulation, validating its role in late acylsugar acetylation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes / vegetative tissue surface exudates

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides an evolutionary intermediate enzyme for reconstructing ancestral acylsugar pathways and comparing acceptor specificity. Useful for pathway engineering when a strict ASAT2-type activity is desired without sucrose acylation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates mono-acylsucrose to the di-acylated product but showed no detectable sucrose-to-mono-acylsucrose activity under standard assay conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports acylsugar biosynthesis as an ASAT2-type enzyme and helps define the evolutionary timing of substrate-specificity shifts in Solanaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog identification by phylogeny/BLAST; in vitro enzyme assays with aiC6 and nC12 acyl-CoA donors; LC/MS product analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate precursor to more highly acylated acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard in vitro assay conditions only; no in planta perturbation reported.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as ortholog in Hyoscyamus niger; expression details not fully elaborated beyond pathway comparison.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mainly to infer evolutionary transition from ancestral ASAT2 to tomato-like ASAT1 activity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Mono-acylsucrose; aiC6-CoA or nC12-CoA donors tested
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HnASAT2 behaved like an ASAT2 ortholog, acylating mono-acylsucrose rather than sucrose. 2. It lacked detectable ASAT1-like activity in the assay conditions used. 3. This supports the inference that the ancestral aASAT2 function persisted until at least the Atropina-Solaneae common ancestor.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing the evolutionary shift toward tomato-like ASAT1 activity and for engineering direct sucrose acylation in Solanum backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates unsubstituted sucrose to form mono-acylsucrose, producing products such as S1:6(6) and S1:12(12) from sucrose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports direct entry of sucrose into acylsugar biosynthesis and marks the lineage where aASAT2 orthologs had shifted to ASAT1-like function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog identification; in vitro enzyme assays with sucrose and acyl-CoAs; LC/MS product analysis in evolutionary comparison.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Commits sucrose into acylsugar pathway as first acylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard in vitro assay conditions only.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified in S. nigrum transcriptome; detailed tissue pattern not specified here.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly to demonstrate that by the S. nigrum-S. lycopersicum divergence, the aASAT2 ortholog lineage had adopted sucrose acylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnASAT1 directly acylated sucrose to mono-acylsucrose in vitro. 2. Its activity resembles tomato SlASAT1 rather than ancestral ASAT2 orthologs from earlier-diverging taxa. 3. This supports a functional evolutionary shift toward ASAT1-like activity before divergence of S. nigrum and cultivated tomato.
